key_insight: A hybrid phase-field plus attention-based deep learning framework can accurately and efficiently predict 2D and 3D ternary alloy microstructure evolution across early- and late-stage spinodal decomposition.
engineering_relevance: This enables faster, physics-informed design and optimization of nanoporous and bicontinuous materials by reducing the computational cost of long-time microstructure simulations.
method: Autoencoder compresses 256×256×3 microstructure fields into compact latent representations.
Attention-enhanced ConvLSTM learns spatiotemporal evolution patterns from phase-field simulation data.
Slice-by-slice extension generalizes the workflow to 3D systems (128×128×128×3) and a hybrid strategy uses PFM for early stages and DL for late-stage coarsening.
evidence_signal: The abstract reports accurate prediction up to 400 timesteps ahead and successful generalization to compositions outside the training distribution.
watch_item: Generalization may depend on the quality and diversity of phase-field training data, especially when extending slice-based 3D predictions to new alloy compositions or regimes.
paper_title: Bridging Phase-Field Model and Deep Learning for Predicting 2D and 3D Microstructure Evolution in Ternary Alloys
